name: Collformic and Santa Fe del Montseny Loop
name_original: Cardedeu y Sant Celoni desde Centelles — circular por el Parque Natural del Montseny
description: This difficult 64.5-mile road cycling route through Montseny Natural Park offers 6650 feet of climbing and stunning views.
region_intro: Road cycling around Cardedeu offers diverse landscapes and challenging terrain, benefiting from its strategic location near the Montseny Natural Park, a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve. The region features varied topography, including the Corredor Mountain Range and several mountain passes like Parpers Pass, providing options for different fitness levels. Cyclists can navigate through pine and holm oak forests on well-paved surfaces, with routes ranging from less strenuous rides to demanding ascents. Best road cycling routes around Cardedeu The most popular road cycling route is Collformic and Santa Fe del Montseny Loop, a 64.5 miles (103.7 km) trail that takes 5 hours 39 minutes to complete. This difficult route traverses the Montseny Natural Park, offering significant climbing. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Fountain – Canyamars Town Center loop from Cardedeu, a moderate 27.8 miles (44.8 km) path. This route provides a balanced ride with varied scenery. Local road cyclists also love the Cànoves – Festival de Mareta loop from Cardedeu, a 33.3 miles (53.5 km) trail leading through the local countryside, often completed in about 2 hours 27 minutes. Road cycling around Cardedeu is defined by the Montseny Natural Park, the Corredor Mountain Range, and various mountain passes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to difficult climbs. The routes in Cardedeu are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from more than 30 reviews. More than 2000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Cardedeu's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Cardedeu?
A: There are over 10 road cycling routes around Cardedeu, offering a diverse range of experiences. These include options for various fitness levels, from moderate rides to challenging ascents through the region's natural parks.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes near Cardedeu?
A: Road cycling around Cardedeu features diverse landscapes, from the challenging terrain of the Montseny Natural Park, a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve, to the pine and holm oak forests of the Corredor Mountain Range. You'll encounter well-paved surfaces and mountain passes like the Parpers Pass, offering varied gradients and scenic views.

Q: Are there any challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, experienced riders will find several challenging routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983244084/collformic-and-santa-fe-del-montseny-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collformic and Santa Fe del Montseny Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 64.5-mile route through Montseny Natural Park with over 6,600 feet of climbing. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/26830003" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Revell Pass – Viladrau loop from Cardedeu&lt;/a&gt;, which also requires very good fitness.

Q: Are there any moderate road cycling routes around Cardedeu?
A: Absolutely. For those seeking a balanced ride, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31363662" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fountain – Canyamars Town Center loop from Cardedeu&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate 27.8-mile path with varied scenery. Another great choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15256676" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cànoves – Festival de Mareta loop from Cardedeu&lt;/a&gt;, a 33.3-mile trail through the local countryside.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or attractions I can see along the road cycling routes?
A: Many routes offer views of significant landmarks. You can cycle near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/913262" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parpers Pass&lt;/a&gt;, which winds through forests, or explore areas close to the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1452213" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Corredor Sanctuary&lt;/a&gt;. Some trails also offer glimpses of historical sites like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1126577" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Burriac Castle&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any loop road cycling routes available from Cardedeu?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes around Cardedeu are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983244084/collformic-and-santa-fe-del-montseny-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collformic and Santa Fe del Montseny Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31363663" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Can Bordoi Pass loop from Cardedeu&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Cardedeu?
A: The road cycling routes in Cardedeu are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 30 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, the stunning natural beauty of Montseny Natural Park, and the well-paved surfaces that make for an enjoyable ride.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Cardedeu?
A: Cardedeu is suitable for road cycling for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. While winter routes are available, it's always advisable to check local weather conditions, especially when planning rides through higher elevations in Montseny Natural Park.

Q: Are there any road cycling routes that pass through mountain passes?
A: Yes, the region around Cardedeu is known for its mountain passes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/913262" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parpers Pass&lt;/a&gt; is a popular, gentle climb that connects to many other routes in the coastal mountain range. More challenging passes are featured in routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/26830003" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Revell Pass – Viladrau loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I find routes that offer views of natural parks?
A: Many road cycling routes in Cardedeu provide stunning views of the surrounding natural parks. The Montseny Natural Park, a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve, is a prominent feature, with routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983244084/collformic-and-santa-fe-del-montseny-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collformic and Santa Fe del Montseny Loop&lt;/a&gt; traversing its beautiful landscapes. You can also experience the natural beauty of the Montnegre y el Corredor Natural Park.

Q: What is the typical duration for a road cycling route around Cardedeu?
A: The duration of road cycling routes around Cardedeu varies significantly based on distance and difficulty. For example, a moderate route like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15256676" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cànoves – Festival de Mareta loop&lt;/a&gt; can be completed in about 2 hours 27 minutes, while a difficult route such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983244084/collformic-and-santa-fe-del-montseny-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collformic and Santa Fe del Montseny Loop&lt;/a&gt; typically takes around 5 hours 39 minutes.
tips: Straight with the whole of Montseny where you can see El turó de l'home, Collformic, and all the mountains of the Natural Park in the background.

A beautiful point to rest and appreciate the views of the Collformic.

On days with good visibility you can also see the Pyrenees.

The Church of Sant Jaume de Viladrover is a Romanesque work in Brull (Osona) protected as a Cultural Property of Local Interest.

   Sant Jaume de Viladrover or dels Bastons is isolated, near a farmhouse, 2 km from Balenyà. It is a one-nave building, covered with a barrel vault with lunettes and toral arches, topped by a semicircular apse decorated with arches under a sawtooth frieze built at the beginning of the 20th century. On the north side, the body of the sacristy was added. It preserves the original door in the south wall, with a semi-circular arch with two-tone shutters, all of which have been blinded. The current access is on the west facade where there is a rectangular door under a 17th century semicircular pediment and a cross-shaped window. The most remarkable thing is the slightly rhomboid bell tower, three stories high, which rests on the vault at the west end of the nave. Despite the modifications from the Baroque era, it preserves some of the twined Romanesque windows with column and crescent capital and the decoration of arches under the Lombard-type saw-tooth frieze.
The road is a little broken up and rocky for about 100 meters. After that it turns into a narrow road which is perfect for cycling.

faqs.question: How challenging is the Cardedeu and Sant Celoni from Centelles circular route?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as a difficult road ride, demanding very good fitness. It involves significant sustained effort, including a first-category climb comparable to many renowned Tour de France cols. Cyclists should be prepared for a strenuous effort over its 104-kilometer distance and 2,030 meters of elevation gain.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route is predominantly road cycling, traversing the diverse landscapes of the Montseny Natural Park. You'll encounter gentle hills, steep ascents, and notable mountain passes. While many roads are well-surfaced, some segments, particularly the final 5-6 kilometers leading to Turó de l'Home, may be unpaved with broken asphalt and steep gradients, resembling more of a path. The ascent from Sant Celoni to Santa Fe de Montseny features 18 hairpin turns with sustained, demanding gradients.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest or viewpoints along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers stunning natural landscapes and several notable points. You'll pass a Straight section with views of Montseny and enjoy a Panoramic view from the Collformic road. The Sant Jaume de Viladrover Church is another highlight. A 4-kilometer detour to Turó de l'Home offers rewarding panoramic views of the Mediterranean coast and the Pyrenees massif.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Montseny Natural Park offers beautiful scenery that varies greatly with the seasons. While specific recommendations for this route aren't provided, generally, spring and autumn offer milder temperatures and vibrant landscapes, making them ideal for challenging rides. Summer can be hot, especially on exposed sections, while winter may bring colder temperatures and potential for snow at higher elevations.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route is circular and starts from Centelles. While specific parking details for cyclists in Centelles are not provided, it's a common starting point. You may find parking options in the town center or near the main access roads. Cardedeu and Sant Celoni are also towns along the route where parking might be available if you choose to start elsewhere.

faqs.question: How can I reach the starting points by public transport?
faqs.answer: Centelles, Cardedeu, and Sant Celoni are all accessible by public transport. Centelles is served by the R3 Rodalies de Catalunya train line, connecting it to Barcelona. Cardedeu is on the R2 Nord line, and Sant Celoni is on the R2 Nord and R11 lines. These train services allow cyclists to transport their bikes, making them viable options for reaching the starting points.

faqs.question: Are there any specific regulations or permits required for cycling in Montseny Natural Park?
faqs.answer: Montseny Natural Park is a protected area and UNESCO Biosphere Reserve. While general cycling on established roads is usually permitted, it's always advisable to check the official park regulations for any specific rules regarding cycling, especially off-road or in sensitive areas. No specific permits for road cycling on this route are mentioned, but respecting the natural environment is paramount.

faqs.question: Is this road cycling route suitable for families or beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is explicitly classified as a difficult road ride requiring very good fitness. With over 100 kilometers and 2,000 meters of elevation gain, it is not suitable for beginners or family outings. It includes challenging climbs comparable to professional race stages, making it best suited for experienced road cyclists.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or restaurants along the route for refreshments?
faqs.answer: The route passes through several towns including Centelles, Cardedeu, and Sant Celoni, where you will find various cafes, restaurants, and pubs. Additionally, the Montseny Natural Park area is popular, and there are likely establishments in villages like Santa Fe de Montseny, which is a significant point on the route, where you can refuel.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route partially overlaps with several other paths. Notable intersections include sections of the [CIMA BA15] Collformic (13%), Tourdera (12%), [CIMA BA04] Turó de l'Home (11%), and [CIMA BA02] Santa Fe (4%). These intersections highlight the route's integration into the broader cycling network of the Montseny region.

faqs.question: What should I consider bringing for this challenging ride?
faqs.answer: Given the route's difficulty and length, you should bring a road bike equipped with climbing gears. Essential items include sufficient water and nutrition, repair kits (spare tubes, pump, multi-tool), appropriate cycling attire for varying weather conditions, and a fully charged phone. Due to the sustained effort, a GPS device for navigation is also highly recommended.
